title: Two-layer auth: on_mount + per-event ownership check
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/invoices_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InvoicesLive). Use on_mount {MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :ensure_authenticated} at the top. In handle_event("cancel", %{"id" => id}, socket), look up the invoice and only cancel it if invoice.customer_id == socket.assigns.current_user.id. Deny with put_flash otherwise. mount/3 returns {:ok, assign(socket, :current_user, %{id: 1})} (the :ensure_authenticated hook populates it in real code).
tier_rationale: Mount-level + event-level auth is a multi-site change. Canonical Phoenix auth pattern.
research_citation: Phoenix LiveView Security considerations + Hashrocket authorization post
